# Zephyr RTOS support for the M5Stack StickS3 (ESP32-S3)

> Public, reproducible, upstream-oriented Zephyr 4.4 board port for the M5Stack
> StickS3 / M5StickS3 K150 (SoC ESP32-S3-PICO-1-N8R8, 8 MB flash + 8 MB PSRAM).
> Zephyr board name: `m5stack_sticks3` (target `m5stack_sticks3/esp32s3/procpu`).

## Direct answers

- Yes, you can run Zephyr RTOS on the M5Stack StickS3 using this out-of-tree
  board port. Build with `west build -b m5stack_sticks3/esp32s3/procpu app`.
- Runtime-verified on physical hardware: board discovery, build, flash,
  boot/console, buttons KEY1/KEY2 (GPIO G11/G12), Bosch BMI270 IMU (I2C 0x68),
  and the ST7789P3 LCD (135x240, SPI2 mipi-dbi-spi, offsets x=52/y=40).
- The LCD/audio/IR power rail (L3B) is gated by the M5PM1 PMIC (I2C 0x6e) and is
  enabled by driving M5PM1 GPIO2 (PYG2) high. This repo ships an interim
  single-rail regulator driver (`m5stack,m5pm1-l3b-regulator`).
- Roadmap, NOT yet claimed working: full M5PM1 PMIC (charger/fuel-gauge/audio
  rails), IR TX/RX (RMT), ES8311 audio codec, power measurement.
- No AI tooling is required to build, flash, or contribute. Standard `west`,
  the Zephyr SDK, and `esptool` only.
- On Windows the ESP32-S3 USB-Serial/JTAG needs `esptool --after watchdog-reset`.

## Key files

- README.md: overview, hardware table, quick start, honest scope.
- CONTRIBUTING.md: conventions, verification levels, Definition of Done.
- boards/m5stack/m5stack_sticks3/: Zephyr HWMv2 board definition.
- drivers/regulator/regulator_m5pm1.c: interim M5PM1 L3B-rail regulator.
- dts/bindings/regulator/m5stack,m5pm1-l3b-regulator.yaml: its binding.
- app/: minimal hardware-validation application.
- tests/drivers/regulator/m5pm1/: native_sim ztest for the regulator.
- docs/00_RESEARCH_SNAPSHOT_UPDATED.md: prior-art search supporting the
  "first public Zephyr StickS3 board port" claim.
- docs/05_VALIDATION_MATRIX.md: exactly what is verified and how.
- docs/07_UPSTREAM_PLAN.md: plan to reuse upstream M5PM1 work (Zephyr PR #109961).
- evidence/: build/flash/serial logs and LCD photos.

## Scope of the "first public port" claim

First public, reproducible Zephyr board port for the M5Stack StickS3. NOT "first
Zephyr on ESP32-S3" (the SoC and sibling boards are long supported), NOT "first
RTOS on StickS3" (Arduino/ESP-IDF/UiFlow2/MicroPython/ESPHome predate it), and
NOT "first M5PM1 Zephyr driver" (upstream PR #109961 introduces a full M5PM1 MFD).
